tluaGenericPackErrors.tlua(5,33): error TLUA100043: A generic type parameter cannot follow a generic pack parameter.
tluaGenericPackErrors.tlua(9,35): error TLUA100044: A generic pack parameter can only be used in a vararg parameter or a multiple-return position.
tluaGenericPackErrors.tlua(10,36): error TLUA100044: A generic pack parameter can only be used in a vararg parameter or a multiple-return position.
tluaGenericPackErrors.tlua(11,43): error TLUA100044: A generic pack parameter can only be used in a vararg parameter or a multiple-return position.
tluaGenericPackErrors.tlua(15,17): error TLUA100045: A generic pack parameter is only allowed on a function or a type alias.
tluaGenericPackErrors.tlua(27,43): error TLUA100046: A generic pack parameter cannot have a constraint or a default.
tluaGenericPackErrors.tlua(28,35): error TLUA100046: A generic pack parameter cannot have a constraint or a default.
tluaGenericPackErrors.tlua(41,12): error TLUA2345: Argument of type 'string' is not assignable to parameter of type 'number'.


==== tluaGenericPackErrors.tlua (8 errors) ====
    // The grammar and usage rules that keep a generic pack parameter in a pack
    // position.
    
    // A pack parameter must trail the plain ones: a plain parameter cannot follow it.
    declare function ordering<...A, T>(...: A): T;
                                    ~
!!! error TLUA100043: A generic type parameter cannot follow a generic pack parameter.
    
    // A pack parameter names several values, so it cannot stand in a single-value
    // position: as a plain annotation, a tuple/return element, or a value type.
    declare function asValue<...A>(x: A): void;
                                      ~
!!! error TLUA100044: A generic pack parameter can only be used in a vararg parameter or a multiple-return position.
    declare function asReturn<...A>(): A;
                                       ~
!!! error TLUA100044: A generic pack parameter can only be used in a vararg parameter or a multiple-return position.
    declare function inList<...A>(): (number, A);
                                              ~
!!! error TLUA100044: A generic pack parameter can only be used in a vararg parameter or a multiple-return position.
    
    // A pack parameter is allowed on an alias, but not an interface or class.
    type Alias<...A> = number;
    interface Iface<...A> {}
                    ~~~~
!!! error TLUA100045: A generic pack parameter is only allowed on a function or a type alias.
    
    // Any function-shaped signature carries one, though: an interface METHOD or call
    // signature is a function, so neither of these errors.
    interface WithMethods {
        m<...A>(...: A): (...A);
        <...R>(...: R): (...R);
    }
    
    // A written constraint or default on a pack parameter is rejected: the implicit
    // constraint is the open `(...any)` pack, and silently ignoring the syntax would
    // enforce nothing.
    declare function constrained<...A extends string>(...: A): void;
                                              ~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA100046: A generic pack parameter cannot have a constraint or a default.
    declare function defaulted<...A = string>(...: A): void;
                                      ~~~~~~
!!! error TLUA100046: A generic pack parameter cannot have a constraint or a default.
    
    // `...T` where T is an ordinary (non-pack) type parameter stays a homogeneous
    // pack of element T -- this is the Stage 1 form and is NOT an error.
    declare function homogeneous<T>(...: T): (...T);
    local okA, okB = homogeneous(1, 2);
    
    // A well-formed pack function, so the errors above are isolated.
    declare function fine<...A, ...R>(f: (...: A) => (...R), ...: A): (...R);
    
    // Explicit scalar arguments are lifted even when a failed call uses its
    // candidate signature for error recovery.
    declare function id<...A>(...: A): (...A);
    id<number>("s");
               ~~~
!!! error TLUA2345: Argument of type 'string' is not assignable to parameter of type 'number'.
